construct_threats: Open interpretive items have no single correct answer — scorer drift if treated as keyed responses.
EMIT numeric fields depend on each pre-service teacher's CODAP choices (variables, thresholds, trees).
evidence_limitations: Interpretive sections require semantic scoring on rubric components, not example-answer matching.
EMIT items (B, C, D, F): full credit on internal arithmetic consistency only — no fixed TP/FP/TN/FN answer key.
Single-level trees (Sections B–C) and two-level tree (Section D) use same complementary operator rule as unplugged WS5/WS6.
Train/test comparison (F) may need log corroboration when available.

cross_worksheet_dependencies.reason: Unplugged threshold grid on 11 classroom cards — same operator pairing (≤↔>, <↔≥) as CODAP single-level trials.
Unplugged çift seviyeli tree on same 11 cards — parallel construct to Section D two-level EMIT tree.
